title: Karmelda
description: This modpack improve your vanilla experience
body:
### **Karmelda**

This modpack is designed to deliver an experience focused on exploration, quality of life, and visual improvements, all while maintaining solid performance from the very first launch. The goal is to provide a stable and complete adventure without requiring any additional configuration.
World generation is completely reimagined through **Terralith**, which expands and diversifies biomes and landscapes to make exploration more engaging and immersive. To enhance the sense of scale, **Distant Horizons** enables long‑distance terrain rendering, allowing players to appreciate the world in greater depth.
Mobility and vertical exploration are improved with **Paragliders**, a gliding system inspired by adventure games that makes it easier to traverse mountains, cliffs, and large structures without disrupting game balance.
On the quality‑of‑life side, essential tools like **Jade**, which provides contextual information about blocks and entities, and **AppleSkin**, which displays clear hunger and saturation indicators, help streamline gameplay and reduce friction. Inventory management is expanded through **Sophisticated Backpacks**, offering upgradeable backpacks with various utilities, while **SecurityCraft** introduces advanced protection systems, cameras, locks, and security mechanisms for bases and builds.
For builders, **Macaw’s Furniture** adds decorative options that enrich construction aesthetics without affecting progression.
Visually, the modpack includes modern improvements to models and entities, along with **Complementary Reimagined** shaders as an optional enhancement for players seeking a more cinematic look. Preconfigured client and server settings are included to ensure stability and performance in both singleplayer worlds and small servers.



_To play easy with your friend this modpack includes e4mc mod_

### **How to use the e4mc mod?**

Simply install the mod, and Open to LAN as normal. A public domain will appear alongside the local port. Others can simply connect to the public domain to connect to your LAN server.
